div.div: Notes — why Docker / ECR / EC2 pulls instead of building locally

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 398px; height: 1px; padding-top: 632px; margin-left: 1442px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
• Docker — same image runs in local dev, CI, and EC2; eliminates "works on my machine" drift.
• ECR — private, region-local registry; EC2 pulls over the AWS network (fast, no public egress) and IAM gates who can push/pull.
• EC2 pulls a pre-built image rather than building in place — keeps the free-tier t3.micro's limited CPU/RAM for running the API, not compiling it.
